frameworks.name: normative evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: decisions follow normative principles for weighing and accumulating sensory observations

frameworks.name: evidence weighting under correlated observations
frameworks.one_line: extends normative accumulation to account for pairwise correlations among observations

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: visual-discrimination task framework for deliberative choice

core_question: Do people adapt evidence weighting and accumulation to correlations among sensory observations in a normative way?
key_assumptions: deliberative decisions follow normative weighting and accumulation principles
correlations among observations alter the evidence each observation provides
adaptation can occur without explicit cues about correlation
manipulations: trial-by-trial changes in pairwise correlations between observations
uncued shifts in the correlation structure
key_findings: participants adapted to uncued, trial-by-trial changes in correlations
decisions reflected approximately normative weighting and accumulation of evidence
minimal training sufficed for adaptation to correlated observations
interpretations: normative principles of evidence weighting extend to correlated sensory environments
human deliberation flexibly accounts for the dependence structure of observations
